message:
test(fsqlite-core): track_w_gate — truth matrix + mixed-OLTP regression gate (bd-aaiwu)

New `crates/fsqlite-core/tests/track_w_gate.rs` (895 lines) adds the
regression gate for the "Track W" effort. Covers six areas laid out
at the top of the file:

1. INSERT lane classifier truth matrix. Each covered INSERT shape is
   asserted to route to either the `DirectCompiled` or the
   `ReusableTableProgram` lane, with explicit coverage of the
   boundary cases (single-row literal, multi-row literal with
   uniform column set, RETURNING-bearing INSERT, INSERT OR REPLACE,
   INSERT INTO … SELECT, parameterized INSERT).
2. UPDATE / DELETE fast-lane verification with fallback
   attribution. Confirms the fast-path counters tick on the
   expected shapes and that fallback routes attribute to the
   correct lane label when the fast path declines.
3. Mixed OLTP regression. Interleaves INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE /
   SELECT calls against the same connection and a second reader
   connection, then asserts lane-usage counters + row visibility.
4. W1 — function-cache preservation on the reusable lane at the
   integration level (not just the unit-test micro case).
5. W9 — retained-autocommit overlay correctness: same-connection
   read-after-write sees the just-committed row without an explicit
   refresh.
6. Cross-connection correctness for prepared DML, to make sure the
   Track W fast-path optimizations haven't reintroduced any
   stale-snapshot behavior on the reader side.

Uses a process-wide `W_GATE_PROFILE_LOCK` + `WGateProfileGuard`
so concurrent `cargo test` workers can't corrupt each other's
`hot_path_profile` readings. The header explicitly asks for
`--test-threads=1 --nocapture` when debugging individual scenarios,
but the mutex keeps the suite correct under the default parallel
runner too.

This is a pure test addition — no production code changes. The gate
is intended to be the single point of failure for any Track W
regression, both for local runs and for CI's pre-merge harness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
